text.title: product-operations Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-operations as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Automating an unstable process in the first 30 days of a new product or team.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automating before workflows stabilize locks in the wrong shape. Wait for at least one full delivery cycle before encoding any process as an agent flow.

failure-mode.description: Agent metric definitions that re-derive "DAU" from prose instead of reading pinned SQL.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate metric definitions. Always pin metric SQL in metric_kpi.definition_sql; never let the agent re-derive a definition from narrative context.

failure-mode.description: Status-update theatre: AI-generated status updates that no one reads.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tie every automated post to a named consumer, or do not generate it. Signal-to-noise collapse kills trust in the entire ops function.

failure-mode.description: Roadmap merge conflicts when nightly agent sync overwrites a concurrent PM edit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use updated_at watermarks and conflict logs, not last-write-wins. Cross-system ID collisions (same feature in Productboard, Jira, and Linear) require an explicit mapping table.

failure-mode.description: Scheduled rollups using AskUserQuestion in a headless cron context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cron jobs have no human; design every automation as headless-first with Slack-based async confirmations for write actions.

failure-mode.description: Unbounded historical pulls without a since/limit/max_pages guard.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Show me all incidents this quarter" silently fetches tens of thousands of tickets and blows the context window. Always pass explicit bounds.

failure-mode.description: Privacy / PII leakage when customer names, emails, support transcripts in feedback tools touch agent context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Strip PII at sync time before the agent context ingests product feedback. This is non-negotiable in regulated environments.

failure-mode.description: Silent webhook drops from Linear/Productboard that the agent never detects.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a heartbeat ticket per source: "Source X last delivered N seconds ago." Without visibility of webhook failures, dashboards silently decay.

failure-mode.description: Agents promoted past their charter, e.g. a status-pack agent deciding to close a feature or rename a KPI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bound write scopes per agent in code, not just in the prompt. Enforce these bounds at the orchestrator layer.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated metric definitions: agents re-derive "DAU = users who logged in in 24h" but the team's actual definition excludes internal users and bots.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always pin metric SQL in metric_kpi.definition_sql; never let the agent re-derive a definition from prose. Cross-check against the canonical schema.

failure-mode.description: Roadmap merge conflicts when two PMs edit the same Productboard feature in parallel and the agent's nightly sync overwrites one.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use updated_at watermarks and conflict logs, not last-write-wins. Cross-system ID collisions (same feature in Productboard, Jira, Linear) require an explicit mapping table.

failure-mode.description: Stakeholder summary tone drift: agents reflexively produce "all-green" digests because they pattern-match on past on-track packs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inject a hard rule: every status pack must surface at least 1 risk or explicitly state "no risks identified, list of checks performed: ...".

failure-mode.description: OKR cargo-culting: LLMs love producing OKR docs but are bad at saying "this OKR is meaningless, kill it."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pair OKR generation with a critique pass and require human sign-off on the kill list before committing.

failure-mode.description: Source-of-truth fragmentation: roadmap in Productboard, sprint in Jira, OKRs in Lattice, retros in Notion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An agent that "centralises metrics" without a canonical schema produces a different set of numbers than the PMs see in their own tools, and trust collapses.

failure-mode.description: Role-clarity gap: agents accelerate delivery but cannot resolve "who owns what."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the human Product Ops charter is undefined, automating it codifies confusion. Define roles before building agents.

failure-mode.description: Status-update theatre: AI-generated status updates that no one reads waste tokens and kill signal-to-noise.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tie every automated post to a named consumer, or do not generate it. Status updates without a known reader are pure waste.

failure-mode.description: Predictive capacity planning is fragile: "AI predicts sprint completion" only works on a stable team with 6+ months of clean ticket history.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: New squads, churn, scope volatility break the model — and bad predictions are worse than none. Wait for baseline stability before automation.

failure-mode.description: Maturity-model jumps fail: skipping Level 1 (process docs) to Level 3 (AI automation).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automating undocumented chaos means you automate the wrong shape. Agents must enforce maturity progression, not bypass it.

failure-mode.description: API rate limits at scale: "weekly portfolio rollup" across 20 squads in Jira hits rate limits fast.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without caching + incremental sync the job times out. Use webhooks over polling; implement exponential backoff.
